Practical Exercises

How to Use This Workbook
01
EXERCISE SLIDES

Each lab has numbered step-by-step instructions taken directly from the AviSuite UA User Manual. Follow every step in order — do not skip ahead.

02
VERIFICATION SLIDES

After each exercise, a green VERIFY slide shows exactly what to check. Confirm all items before moving on. Ask your instructor if anything looks different.

03
EXAMPLE DATA BOXES

Dark blue panels show sample data to type in. Use these exact values unless your instructor gives different ones. Fields in [brackets] need your own input.

04
TIPS & MANUAL REFS

Amber TIP boxes highlight common mistakes. Small chips like “Manual §3.2.2” point to the exact section in the AviSuite UA User Manual.

AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation2
LAB
0
Getting Started
Launch appIdentify windowCheck connectionMessage Office
WHAT YOU WILL PRACTISE
›
Double-click the AMHS User Agent icon on the desktop to launch the application.
›
Identify the 8 menus in the menu bar: File | Messaging | Flight Plan | NOTAM | Tools | Options | Window | Help.
›
Verify the connection status indicator in the bottom-right corner is green.
›
Open Message Office (Messaging > Message Office) and explore the 3-pane layout.
›
Click the Unprocessed folder to view incoming messages.
AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation3
Lab 0: Getting Started
Launch & identify the main window
EXERCISEManual §1.1–§1.4
1
Double-click the AMHS User Agent icon on the desktop.
A splash screen may appear briefly. The main window opens automatically.
2
Check the menu bar — it must show exactly 8 menus:
File | Messaging | Flight Plan | NOTAM | Tools | Options | Window | Help
3
Look at the connection status indicator (bottom-right of the window).
Green = connected. Red or orange = not connected — alert your instructor before continuing.
4
Open Message Office: Messaging > Message Office.
Window shows 3 areas: folder tree (left), message list (centre), preview pane (bottom).
5
Click the Unprocessed folder in the folder tree.
The message list updates. Any incoming messages appear here.
6
When you are done for today: File > Exit to close the UA.
Never shut down the computer while the UA is still running.
AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation4
Lab 0: Verification — What to Check
VERIFY
VERIFY — confirm each item
✓
Main window opened without any error messages.
✓
Menu bar shows all 8 menus in order.
File | Messaging | Flight Plan | NOTAM | Tools | Options | Window | Help
✓
Connection status indicator is green.
✓
Message Office opened showing 3 panes.
Folder tree left / message list centre / preview bottom.
✓
Clicking Unprocessed updated the message list in the centre pane.
⚠

Each AviUnit can only be opened once. Closing an AviUnit window hides it — reopening restores its last state. This is normal behaviour.

AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation5
LAB
1
Sending a Message
ComposeSet priorityAdd recipientsDetails tabSend & verify
WHAT YOU WILL PRACTISE
›
Open the AMHS compose window: Messaging > AMHS.
›
Select ATS Priority KK (Administrative — routine) — see the priority table.
›
Add a recipient address by right-clicking the Recipients field and choosing Add.
›
Set Originator Report Request to “Always” on the Details tab.
›
Send the message and confirm it appears in the Transmitted folder.
AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation6
Lab 1: Composing & Sending an AMHS Message
Compose, prioritise, address, and send
EXERCISEManual §3.2
1
Click Messaging > AMHS to open the compose window.
2
In ATS Prio, select KK (Administrative — routine).
All five priority levels are shown in the table at right.
3
Right-click the Recipients field and choose Add.
Enter the test address from your instructor (e.g. VTBBZTZX in AFTN format).
4
Click the Details tab. Set Originator Report Request to “Always”.
Requests a delivery confirmation when the message is received.
5
Click the Message tab and type a short message in the body area.
6
Click File > Send (or the Send toolbar button).
7
In Message Office, click Transmitted to confirm the message was sent.
ATS PRIORITY LEVELS
SSDistress — highest
DDUrgency
FFFlight safety
GGMeteorological / AIS
KKAdministrative — routine
AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation7
Lab 1: Verification — What to Check
VERIFY
VERIFY — confirm each item
✓
AMHS compose window opened from Messaging > AMHS.
✓
ATS Priority was set to KK before sending.
✓
A recipient address was entered in the Recipients field.
✓
Originator Report Request on the Details tab was set to “Always”.
✓
No error dialog appeared after clicking Send.
✓
The message appears in Transmitted with a correct timestamp.
✓
A delivery report (receipt) arrived in the Unprocessed folder.
This confirms the Originator Report Request setting worked.
AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation8
LAB
2
Using Templates
NOTAM templateFPL templateQ-CodeFieldsSend Over AMHS
WHAT YOU WILL PRACTISE
›
Open the NOTAM template: NOTAM > NOTAM.
›
Fill the Q-Code fields (Q2/3 subject + Q4/5 condition) and Qualifier fields.
›
Complete Item A (location), Item B/C (validity dates), and Item E (text).
›
Open the FPL template: Flight Plan > FPL – Flight Plan.
›
Fill Aircraft ID, Flight Rules, Wake, ADEP, ADES, EOBT, then Send Over AMHS.
AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation9
Lab 2A: Using a TAM Template — NOTAM
Build and send a NOTAM
EXERCISEManual §6.1–§6.2
Part A  —  TAM Templates  ›  NOTAM menu
1
Click NOTAM > NOTAM from the menu bar.
2
Fill Q2/3 (subject) = OB and Q4/5 (condition) = CE.
Combined Q-Code = QOBCE (obstacle, erected).
3
Fill Qualifiers: Traffic=IV · Purpose=NBO · Scope=AE · Lower=000 · Upper=999.
4
Item A: enter your 4-letter ICAO aerodrome code.
Example: VTBB.
5
Item B (begin) and C (end) in YYMMDDHHmm UTC.
B: 2601010600 · C: 2602010600.
6
Item E (free text, mandatory):
CONSTRUCTION CRANE ERECTED. HEIGHT 120M AMSL. LIGHTED.
7
Click File > Send Over AMHS. Add recipients and priority, then Send.
EXAMPLESample NOTAM values
Q) VTBB/QOBCE/IV/NBO/AE/000/999
A) VTBB
B) 2601010600   C) 2602010600
E) CRANE 120M AMSL. LIGHTED.
AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation10
Lab 2A: Verification — NOTAM Template
VERIFY
VERIFY — confirm each item
✓
NOTAM form opened from the NOTAM menu with Q-Code, Qualifier, and Item fields.
✓
Q2/3 and Q4/5 fields accepted 2-letter codes.
✓
Qualifier fields accepted valid values.
Fields with a List of Values show a dropdown. Press F4 to see valid options.
✓
Item A accepted a 4-letter ICAO location code (e.g. VTBB).
✓
Item E accepted a free-text description.
✓
“Send Over AMHS” transferred the NOTAM text to the AMHS compose window.
⚠

Fields using a “List of Values” reject invalid input. Press F4 to open the value dropdown and choose a valid code.

AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation11
Lab 2B: Using an ATS Template — Flight Plan (FPL)
Build and send a flight plan
EXERCISEManual §4.1–§4.2
Part B  —  ATS Templates  ›  Flight Plan menu
1
Click Flight Plan > FPL – Flight Plan.
2
Enter Aircraft ID (max 7 characters): THA601.
3
Set Flight Rules = I (IFR) and Flight Type = S (Scheduled).
Rules: I=IFR V=VFR Y=IFR-first Z=VFR-first · Type: G/M/N/S/X.
4
Set Wake Turbulence Category = M (Medium).
H=Heavy(≥136t) J=A380 M=Medium L=Light(≤7t).
5
Enter ADEP = VTBS and ADES = VDPP (4-letter ICAO).
6
Enter EOBT (Estimated Off-Block Time) in HHmm: 0600.
7
Click File > Send Over AMHS. Add recipients and priority, then Send.
EXAMPLESample FPL values
-THA601/I   -B738/M
-SDE2FGHIRWXY/H
-VTBS0600   -N0450F350 DCT VDPP
-VDPP0120 VTBD
AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation12
Lab 2B: Verification — FPL Template
VERIFY
VERIFY — confirm each item
✓
FPL form opened from Flight Plan > FPL.
✓
Aircraft ID accepted up to 7 characters.
✓
Flight Rules = I and Flight Type = S accepted.
✓
Wake Turbulence Category = M accepted.
✓
ADEP and ADES accepted 4-letter ICAO codes.
✓
“Send Over AMHS” transferred the FPL text to the AMHS compose window.
✓
AMHS compose window showed the correctly formatted FPL text in the body.
⚠

All mandatory FPL fields must be filled before “Send Over AMHS” will transfer the data. Mandatory fields are highlighted in the form.

AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation13
LAB
3
Saving & Managing Templates
Save templateTemplate BrowserLoadDelete
WHAT YOU WILL PRACTISE
›
Open AMHS compose, add a recipient, and type a standard message.
›
Click Template > Save As in the AMHS window menu bar (not File > Save).
›
Enter a name in the dialog and click OK to save the template.
›
Open the Template Browser: Tools > Template Browser.
›
Double-click your template to load it; right-click > Delete to remove it.
AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation14
Lab 3: Saving & Managing Templates
Save, load, and delete templates
EXERCISEManual §3.2.1 / §7.1
1
Open AMHS compose window: Messaging > AMHS.
2
Add a recipient and type a standard message in the body.
Example: ROUTINE ADVISORY. AERODROME OPERATIONS NORMAL.
3
In the AMHS window menu bar: Template > Save As.
This is inside the AMHS window — NOT the main application File menu.
4
Type a name in the dialog and click OK.
Example name: ROUTINE ADVISORY.
5
Close the AMHS window.
6
Main menu > Tools > Template Browser.
All saved templates are listed, grouped by type.
7
Double-click your template to load it into a new compose window.
Pre-filled content should match what you saved in Step 2.
8
Right-click the template > Delete. Confirm it disappears from the list.
⚠

Template > Save As is in the AMHS window’s own menu bar. Manual §3.2.1.2 confirms this path — File > Save does something different.

AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation15
Lab 3: Verification — Saving & Managing
VERIFY
VERIFY — confirm each item
✓
Template > Save As appeared in the AMHS window’s own menu bar.
✓
A name dialog appeared and accepted your template name.
✓
Template Browser (Tools > Template Browser) listed your saved template.
✓
Double-clicking the template opened a compose window with pre-filled content.
Recipient and body should exactly match what you typed in Step 2.
✓
Right-clicking showed a context menu with a Delete option.
✓
After deletion, the template no longer appears in the browser list.
⚠

Template > Load (also in the AMHS window menu) applies a saved template to an already-open compose window without opening the Template Browser.

LAB
4
Receiving & Reading Messages
InboxSS priorityViewReplyNotifications
WHAT YOU WILL PRACTISE
›
Open Message Office and click the Unprocessed folder.
›
Identify SS-priority messages (red background) — these must be read first.
›
Single-click to preview; double-click to open the full message viewer.
›
Right-click > Reply to to compose a reply with the sender pre-filled.
›
Handle pop-up notifications and check for file attachments.
AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation17
Lab 4: Receiving, Reading & Replying
Read, prioritise, and reply
EXERCISEManual §3.1 / §3.1.1.3
1
In Message Office, click the Unprocessed folder.
Incoming messages list in the centre pane. The folder counter shows the unread count.
2
Check for red-highlighted messages — these are SS priority (Distress).
SS messages must always be read before others.
3
Single-click a message to see a preview in the bottom pane.
4
Double-click to open the full viewer.
Or right-click > View (structured) / View as Plain (raw text).
5
Note the header: Originator address, ATS Priority, Filing Time.
6
Right-click > Reply to. The sender address is pre-filled. Add text and Send.
7
If a notification pop-up appears: read it and click OK.
AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation18
Lab 4: Verification — Receiving Messages
VERIFY
VERIFY — confirm each item
✓
Unprocessed folder listed incoming messages in the centre pane.
✓
SS-priority messages (if any) were highlighted with a red background.
✓
Single-clicking showed a preview in the bottom pane without opening a new window.
✓
Double-clicking opened the full viewer with all header fields visible.
Originator, ATS Priority, Filing Time, and message body all visible.
✓
Right-click context menu showed “Reply to” and “Reply All” options.
✓
If a notification appeared, it showed the originator and message reference.
✓
Messages with attachments showed a paper-clip icon — double-click to open/save.
AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation19
LAB
5
Searching Messages
Open SearchDate filterWildcard %OriginatorSave search
WHAT YOU WILL PRACTISE
›
Open Search: Message Office > File > Search Messages.
›
Run a date-range search — set Creation Time to today and click Search.
›
Use the % wildcard in Body Text: %TEST% finds messages containing “TEST”.
›
Use VTBB% in Originator to find messages from addresses starting with VTBB.
›
Save search constraints as a reusable subfolder: File > Save Search Constraints.
AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation20
Lab 5: Searching with Filters & Wildcards
Filter by date, body text, and originator
EXERCISEManual §3.1.1.4
1
Open Search: Message Office > File > Search Messages.
Or double-click the Search Result node in the folder tree.
2
Date search: set Creation Time to today. Leave other fields empty. Click Search.
All messages from today should appear in the result list.
3
Clear all fields. In Body Text type %TEST% and click Search.
% is a wildcard. %TEST% returns every message with “TEST” anywhere in the body.
4
Originator search: type VTBB% in the Originator field and click Search.
Returns all messages from any address starting with the prefix VTBB.
5
Click a result to confirm it opens in the viewer.
6
Click File > Save Search Constraints to save this search.
It appears as a subfolder under Search Result — click it anytime to re-run.
EXAMPLE% wildcard examples
%TEST%  = body contains “TEST”
VTBB%  = originator starts with VTBB
%ZTZX  = originator ends with ZTZX
AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation21
Lab 5: Verification — Searching Messages
VERIFY
VERIFY — confirm each item
✓
Search window opened from File > Search Messages in Message Office.
✓
Date-range search returned all messages from today.
✓
%TEST% returned only messages containing “TEST” in the body.
✓
VTBB% returned only messages from addresses starting with VTBB.
Widen the date range or check the prefix spelling if no results appear.
✓
Clicking a result opened the full message in the viewer.
✓
File > Save Search Constraints created a new subfolder under Search Result.
Double-click the subfolder anytime to re-run the same query instantly.
⚠

Saved searches are useful for recurring checks — e.g. “all messages from VTBB today.” They re-run automatically when you open the folder.

AMHS User Agent Lab Workbook  •  AviSuite UA · Standalone Workstation22
Quick Reference — Menus, Priorities & Wildcards
Keep this slide handy while practising

Priority Levels

SS  Distress (highest)DD  UrgencyFF  Flight safetyGG  Met / AIS / regularityKK  Admin (routine)

Key Menu Paths

New msg: Messaging > AMHSMsg Office: Messaging > MOTemplates: Tools > BrowserSearch: MO > File > SearchExit: File > Exit

Search Wildcards

%  = any characters%TEXT%  = contains TEXTVTBB%  = starts with VTBB%ZX  = ends with ZXuse in Body / Originator

Template Save

In the AMHS window:Template > Save As(not File > Save)Load: Template > LoadBrowse: Tools > Browser
